preamble.summary: The brothers tell Jacob Joseph is alive. God appears at Beer-sheba assuring Jacob. Jacob goes to Egypt with his family. A genealogy lists all seventy souls who went to Egypt.
preamble.remarkable: The genealogical list connects Israel's seventy souls to the Table of Nations' seventy peoples. God's reassurance at Beer-sheba is the last theophany before Sinai.
preamble.friction: The number seventy differs from the Septuagint's seventy-five (Acts 7:14).

verses.text_reference: And Israel took his journey and offered sacrifice at Beer-sheba.
verses.rendering: Israel set out and went to Beer-sheba. He offered a sacrifice to the God of his father Isaac.

verses.text_reference: And the Lord appeared to him: 'Jacob! Jacob!'
verses.rendering: The Lord appeared to Jacob in a vision of the night and called: Jacob! Jacob! He answered: Here I am.

verses.text_reference: 'Fear not to go down into Egypt.'
verses.rendering: He said: I am the God of your fathers. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for I will make you a great nation there.

verses.text_reference: 'I will go down with thee and bring thee up again.'
verses.rendering: I will go down with you, and I will bring you back. And Joseph will close your eyes at death.

verses.text_reference: And he arose and went to Egypt.
verses.rendering: He set out from Beer-sheba on the sixteenth of the third month and journeyed to Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent Judah before him to Joseph.
verses.rendering: Jacob sent Judah ahead to Joseph, to scout the land of Goshen.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph went out to meet Israel.
verses.rendering: Joseph went out to meet his father in Goshen. He fell on his father's neck and wept.

verses.text_reference: And Israel said: 'Now let me die, since I have seen thy face.'
verses.rendering: Israel said: Now I can die, for I have seen your face — you are still alive.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob's sons came with their cattle and possessions.
verses.rendering: Jacob's sons and their sons came with him, bringing their livestock and possessions.

verses.text_reference: And these are the names of the children of Israel who went into Egypt.
verses.rendering: These are the names of the children of Israel who went down to Egypt with their father Jacob.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
verses.rendering: The sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi — four sons.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul.
verses.rendering: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanite woman — six sons.
translator_notes: Shaul's Canaanite mother sits uncomfortably with the intermarriage prohibition of chapter 30.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
verses.rendering: The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari — three sons.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.
verses.rendering: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. Er and Onan had died. The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Pua, Jashub, and Shimron.
verses.rendering: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron — four sons.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
verses.rendering: The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel — three sons.

verses.text_reference: These are the sons of Leah. All her descendants were twenty-nine, with Jacob thirty.
verses.rendering: These are the sons of Leah. The total of her descendants who went to Egypt was twenty-nine, or thirty counting Jacob himself.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Zilpah: Gad and Asher. Gad's sons: seven. Asher's sons and daughter: seven.
verses.rendering: The sons of Zilpah: Gad and Asher. Gad had seven sons. Asher had four sons, one daughter, and two grandsons. Total: fourteen.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
verses.rendering: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

verses.text_reference: Joseph had Manasseh and Ephraim in Egypt.
verses.rendering: Born to Joseph in Egypt: Manasseh and Ephraim — two sons.

verses.text_reference: Benjamin's sons: ten.
verses.rendering: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard — ten sons.

verses.text_reference: Rachel's descendants: fourteen.
verses.rendering: The total of Rachel's descendants was fourteen.

verses.text_reference: The sons of Bilhah: Dan and Naphtali. Total: seven.
verses.rendering: The sons of Bilhah: Dan and Naphtali. Dan had one son; Naphtali had four. Total: seven.

verses.text_reference: All the souls who went to Egypt: seventy.
verses.rendering: All the souls of Jacob who went down to Egypt numbered seventy.

verses.text_reference: And they all dwelt in Goshen.
verses.rendering: They all settled in Goshen. They took possession of it and were fruitful and multiplied.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob was one hundred and thirty when he came into Egypt.
verses.rendering: Jacob was one hundred and thirty years old when he entered Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph nourished his father and brethren.
verses.rendering: Joseph provided for his father, his brothers, and all their households with bread.

verses.text_reference: And the land of Egypt was afflicted by the famine.
verses.rendering: The land of Egypt was devastated. The people sold their possessions to Joseph for food.

verses.text_reference: And the people became bondservants to Pharaoh.
verses.rendering: The people became servants to Pharaoh. Only the priests' land he did not buy.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob lived in Egypt seventeen years.
verses.rendering: Jacob and his sons lived in Goshen, the best part of Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob was one hundred and forty-seven years when he died.
verses.rendering: Jacob lived one hundred and forty-seven years and died in the seventeenth year of the seventh week of the forty-fifth jubilee.

verses.text_reference: And Israel blessed his sons before he died.
verses.rendering: Israel blessed his sons before he died and told them everything that would happen to them in the last days.

verses.text_reference: And he gave Joseph two portions in the land.
verses.rendering: He blessed them and gave Joseph a double portion in the land.
translator_notes: The 'double portion' is the firstborn's right, transferred from Reuben.

verses.text_reference: And he died in the land of Egypt.
verses.rendering: He died in the land of Egypt and was carried up and buried in the double cave in Canaan.
